requirement.original: 现在所有 specialist agents（ui-specialist、architect、product-owner、user）给出的报告在 dev 工作流中与 BA/dev/QA 的 context 传递不连贯，且缺乏 handoff contract。具体表现：1. UI specialist 报告使用自然语言描述视觉问题（如

），但不包含 CSS selector、measured vs expected 值、源代码位置等 BA/dev 可操作的字段。2. 所有 specialist 与 BA 之间没有 handoff contract，BA 收到 specialist findings 后没有规范化的消费协议（哪些字段必须提取、哪些 claim 需要自行验证、遇到模糊描述如何处理）。3. 结果：BA 和 dev 只能盲人摸象，无法根据 specialist 报告做出确定性的实现决策。目标：修改相关 agent spec 文件，在所有 specialist 输出和 BA 消费侧同时建立 handoff contract。
requirement.clarified: Modify skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json (finding_base: structured location, measured, expected, downstream_agent), agents/ui-specialist.md (prose alignment to canonical channels), agents/architect.md (structured location + measured/expected/downstream_agent), agents/product-owner.md (observed_behavior + expected_behavior + downstream_agent), agents/user.md (same as product-owner, location.file nullable), and agents/ba.md (Specialist Findings Intake Protocol section). The machine-enforced ui-specialist contract is report-schema.json; the other 4 files are agent-prose convention.
requirement.what: Bidirectional handoff contract establishment: report-schema.json finding_base enrichment (machine-enforced, ui-specialist) + prose schema enrichment for architect/product-owner/user + BA intake protocol
requirement.why: BA and dev cannot make deterministic implementation decisions from current specialist reports — schemas lack measured/expected values, CSS selectors, source file locations, and routing metadata

included: skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json — finding_base: location changed from flat string to structured object {url,selector,file,line} with nullable fields; measured, expected, downstream_agent added to base; required list updated
agents/ui-specialist.md — prose updated: references canonical channels (automated_findings, contextual_findings, aesthetic_findings); issues[] no longer described as canonical finding channel
agents/architect.md — issues[] location changes from string to structured object {file, line, url?}; gains measured, expected, downstream_agent
agents/product-owner.md — issues[] gains observed_behavior, expected_behavior, downstream_agent
agents/user.md — issues[] gains observed_behavior, expected_behavior, downstream_agent; location.file documented as nullable
agents/ba.md — new Specialist Findings Intake Protocol section
excluded: agents/pm.md — PM triage uses specialist reports but user did not request PM changes
commands/dev.md — orchestrator routing already passes specialist findings to BA; no changes needed

success_criteria: skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json finding_base has structured location {url,selector,file,line} (all nullable), measured, expected, downstream_agent='ba' — all three canonical channels inherit via $ref
agents/ui-specialist.md prose references automated_findings/contextual_findings/aesthetic_findings as canonical channels; does not describe issues[] as a canonical JSON schema channel
architect issues[] location is a structured object {file, line, url?} not a flat string; gains measured, expected, downstream_agent
product-owner issues[] contains observed_behavior, expected_behavior, downstream_agent
user issues[] contains observed_behavior, expected_behavior, downstream_agent with nullable location.file
agents/ba.md contains 'Specialist Findings Intake Protocol' section with mandatory fields, tier_3_tainted handling, measurement fallback, fast-fail rule
commands/dev.md and agents/pm.md are NOT changed
constraints: user agent is browser-only — location.file MUST be nullable
report-schema.json: finding_deterministic computed_value/expected_value MUST be retained (not removed); they are raw tool-output fields complementary to the new base measured/expected string fields
architect location change from string to object is a BREAKING schema change — flag in implementation
All existing fields in each specialist's issues[] must be preserved (additive only)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Step 2 already routes specialist findings to BA — no changes needed; correctly out of scope
PM triage consumes specialist reports; a parallel enrichment of PM consumption protocol could benefit from this same schema change but user did not request it

root_cause_analysis.symptom: BA and dev receive natural-language specialist findings ('button spacing inconsistent') but lack CSS selector, measured vs. expected values, and source file locations needed to make deterministic implementation decisions
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: For ui-specialist: the machine-enforced output contract (skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json, finding_base) has location as a flat string with no selector/file/line decomposition; no measured, expected, or downstream_agent fields exist at any finding level. The issues[] key in ui-specialist.md:255 is a prose example only — not a JSON Schema channel. For architect/product-owner/user: issues[] prose schemas lack structured location, measured/expected, and downstream_agent. BA has no intake protocol for consuming specialist findings.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — architectural gap, not introduced by a specific commit
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Specialist agents were designed with PM-first output in mind; the ui-specialist JSON schema was not extended to include BA-actionable routing fields; BA consumption protocol was never formalized
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: BA cannot apply Contract A (Evidence) without measured/expected values; cannot perform targeted grep without file:line:selector; cannot deterministically route findings without downstream_agent. The issues[] prose in ui-specialist.md misled prior BA analysis to target a non-enforced channel.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Present from initial specialist agent design; no regression
observations: skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json definitions.finding_base lines 214-233: location is type:string (flat string); no measured, expected, downstream_agent, selector fields at any level (inferred: Read)
skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json: required channels are automated_findings, contextual_findings, aesthetic_findings, needs_review, unknowns — no issues[] channel (inferred: Read lines 7-24)
agents/ui-specialist.md line 255: `'issues': []` is inside a prose example block; not a channel defined in report-schema.json (inferred: Read)
agents/architect.md lines 306-318: location field is a flat string 'file:line or module/component name' — no structured object, no measured, no expected (inferred: Read)
agents/product-owner.md lines 327-340: no observed_behavior, no expected_behavior fields present (inferred: Read)
agents/user.md lines 427-442: no observed_behavior, no expected_behavior fields present (inferred: Read)
agents/ba.md: grep for 'Specialist Findings Intake Protocol' returns empty (inferred: grep)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — these are agent spec files and JSON schema files, not running systems; runtime observation is not applicable for this change type)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Direct Read of each agent spec file and report-schema.json confirmed schema deficiencies at specific line numbers
root_cause_analysis.located_source: skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json:214-233 (finding_base), agents/ui-specialist.md:255 (prose example only), agents/architect.md:306-318, agents/product-owner.md:327-340, agents/user.md:427-442, agents/ba.md (no intake protocol section)

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Every issue MUST have `browser_verified: true` AND a screenshot AND a measurement (px / ratio / ms)
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Ensures ui-specialist findings have runtime evidence before reporting

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: You report WHAT you observe and WHERE. You do NOT diagnose WHY or suggest HOW to fix.
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Symptom-Only Reporting constraint — prevents root cause speculation by specialist

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Report the observable symptom with measurements (e.g., 'GET /api/data returns 2340ms')

setup.reason: non-UI -- agent-prompt edit; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: UI specialist 报告使用自然语言描述视觉问题，但不包含 CSS selector、measured vs expected 值、源代码位置等 BA/dev 可操作的字段。所有 specialist 与 BA 之间没有 handoff contract。BA 收到 specialist findings 后没有规范化的消费协议。

evidence.gap: 5 files missing at least one of: structured location object, measured field, expected field, downstream_agent field, intake protocol. All gaps are additive (nothing to remove, only to add).

reference_source.description: SARIF v2.1.0 (OASIS Standard) — industry standard for findings interchange between analysis tools and downstream consumers. Defines location.file, location.selector (physicalLocation), severity, title, description, remediation schemas.

development_approach.strategy: Additive schema enrichment across 4 specialist agent files; new intake protocol section in ba.md. No existing fields removed. architect location field type change from string to object is the only breaking change — flag prominently.
files_to_modify: agents/ui-specialist.md — add inline issues[] per-issue schema with location.{url,selector,file,line}, measured, expected, downstream_agent
agents/architect.md — change location from string to {file,line,url?} object; add measured, expected, downstream_agent
agents/user.md — add observed_behavior, expected_behavior, downstream_agent (location.file nullable)
agents/ba.md — add Specialist Findings Intake Protocol section after Four Contracts, before Forbidden BA Patterns
development_approach.validation_approach: QA verifies: (1) grep for new field names in each file, (2) grep for 'Specialist Findings Intake Protocol' in ba.md, (3) confirm commands/dev.md and agents/pm.md are unchanged, (4) confirm ui-specialist.md still references report-schema.json (not replaced)

queries: CrewAI LangGraph multi-agent message contracts inter-agent communication schema
AutoGen Semantic Kernel agent-to-agent handoff structured output format
SARIF security findings schema agent report contract standards
multi-agent system findings format mandatory fields actionable downstream
agent orchestrator worker agent context passing schema JSON contract
accessibility audit findings schema WCAG axe-core JSON format mandatory fields
key_insights: SARIF v2.1.0 (OASIS standard) is the authoritative format for findings interchange — defines location.file, location.selector (physicalLocation), severity, title, description, remediation; tier_1_external reference for field names
axe-core raw finding schema defines selector, impact, wcagTags, findingType per-DOM-node — confirms selector as a mandatory field for UI findings
Industry consensus from CrewAI/LangGraph/AutoGen/Semantic Kernel: agents pass typed JSON schemas not free-form text; invalid messages fail fast (fast-fail semantics)
Bidirectional contract requirement: upstream agent documents what it produces; downstream agent declares what it accepts — both must match before deployment (GitHub blog on multi-agent failures)
downstreamAgent field pattern used in orchestrator-worker architectures to route findings to correct consuming agent without ambiguity
research_findings.how_applied: SARIF field names (location.file, location.selector, title, description, severity, remediation) adopted as tier_1_external reference for the proposed issues[] schema enrichment. Fast-fail semantics from industry consensus directly shaped the BA intake protocol's mandatory-field rejection rule. downstreamAgent routing field pattern adopted from orchestrator-worker literature.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed schema deficiencies by reading actual agent files. Key findings: (1) ui-specialist.md Output Format section at line 255 shows issues:[] with no inline schema; (2) architect.md location field confirmed as flat string; (3) No 'Specialist Findings Intake Protocol' in ba.md. Codex noted the inline schema approach (additive to report-schema.json) is correct. No expansionist scope was flagged.

findings.issue: ui-specialist issues[] has no inline schema in Output Format — only external delegation to report-schema.json
findings.proposed_fix: Add inline issues[] per-issue schema to Output Format section of ui-specialist.md alongside (not replacing) report-schema.json reference
findings.rationale: Confirmed by Read of agents/ui-specialist.md:255; inline schema required by R1

findings.issue: architect.md location is flat string — not actionable for file:line navigation
findings.proposed_fix: Change location from string to structured object {file, line, url?}; flag as breaking change
findings.rationale: Confirmed by Read of agents/architect.md:306-318; SARIF physicalLocation is structured object

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Both Codex findings are directly encoded in the spec requirements and technical hints. The breaking-change flag for architect.md location type change is explicitly documented.
